member.name: Yvette Cooper
member.role: MP for Pontefract, Castleford and Knottingley

addresses: Queen's Mill, Aire Street, Castleford, WF10 1JL

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Yvette Cooper is the Labour MP for Pontefract, Castleford and Knottingley , and has been an MP continually since 1 May 1997. She currently holds the Government post of Foreign Secretary.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Homes for Ukraine Scheme: Visas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many visa applications under the Homes for Ukraine scheme have currently been outstanding for more than (a) one month, (b) three months and (c) six months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: UKVI aims to assess all Ukraine Schemes visa applications as quickly as possible and straightforward applications are processed within a matter of days. It is right, however, that visas are only issued after all necessary checks have been completed which ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Homes for Ukraine Scheme: Visas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what the average processing time for a Homes for Ukraine Scheme visa was in the latest period for which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: UKVI aims to assess all Ukraine Schemes visa applications as quickly as possible and straightforward applications are processed within a matter of days. It is right, however, that visas are only issued after all necessary checks have been completed which ...

writtenQuestions.heading: National Crime Agency: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, with reference to Statement by the Prime on Illegal Immigration of 13tDecember 202, Official Report column 885, what the total budget of the National Crime Agency for organised immigration crime was i
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NCA’s budget for the 2022-23 financial year is £800 million. The NCA prioritises resources to target offenders operating across a range of serious and organised crime threats, including Organised Immigration Crime.

writtenQuestions.heading: People Smuggling: English Channel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate her Department has made of the number of organised crime groups active in facilitating people smuggling across the English Channel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is resolute in its commitment to tackle Organised Immigration Crime (OIC).Tackling people smuggling of all types is a priority for Immigration Enforcement, working through its international network upstream as well as conducting OIC investi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Human Trafficking: Prosecutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many people smugglers who aid migrants to illegally cross the English Channel have been (a) identified and (b) prosecuted in each month since January 2021.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office aims to tackle the organised crime gangs behind illegal migration. The Home Office works tirelessly, with other national and international law enforcement authorities, to tackle migrant smuggling gangs who organise and profit from illegal ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Finance

writtenQuestions.answerText: The United Kingdom has a statutory obligation to provide destitute asylum seekers with accommodation and other support whilst their application for asylum is being considered.The Departmental allocation for asylum support in 2022/23 main estimates was £10...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how much funding has been allocated by (a) her Department and (b) FCDO in departmental budgets for financial year 2022-23 to cover support costs for people in receipt of asylum support.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The United Kingdom has a statutory obligation to provide destitute asylum seekers with accommodation and other support whilst their application for asylum is being considered.The Departmental allocation for asylum support in 2022/23 main estimates was £10...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what is the average length of time spent by an asylum-seeker waiting for a decision on inadmissibility.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Published statistics on inadmissibility can be found online at https://www.gov.uk/government/statistics/immigration-statistics-year-ending-september-2021/how-many-people-do-we-grant-asylum-or-protection-to#inadmissibilityThe Home Office does not publish a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Hotels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate she has made of the number of asylum-seekers in hotel accommodation awaiting an inadmissibility decision.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office has a statutory obligation to provide accommodation and other support to asylum seekers who would otherwise be destitute while their application for asylum is being considered.The significant increase in dangerous journeys across the Chann...

writtenQuestions.heading: Manston Asylum Processing Centre: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether she has made an assessment of the possible compensation due to asylum-seekers as a result of the conditions at Manston asylum centre.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions to detain people are not taken lightly. We will robustly defend decisions subject to legal challenge, when appropriate to do so, with a decision to defend or concede based on the merits of the individual case.The facility at Manston provides sui...

writtenQuestions.heading: Home Office: Email
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether the draft Written Ministerial Statement emailed by the Home Secretary to the hon. Member for South Holland and the Deepings was market sensitive.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I wrote to the Home Affairs Select Committee Chair on 31 October and a copy of the letter was placed in the House Libraries - I refer the Rt. Hon. member to that letter.
